content:
What Programs Support Early-Stage Ideation?

{/* NSERC Discovery Grants */}
NSERC Discovery Grants Program
Up to $1M/5 years
Basic Research
Multi-Year

Natural Sciences and Engineering Research Council's flagship program supporting ongoing basic research programs with long-term goals, fostering innovation and discovery in natural sciences and engineering.

Research Areas:
  • • Natural sciences and engineering
  • • Fundamental research programs
  • • Long-term research objectives
  • • Novel concepts and methodologies
Program Features:
  • • 5-year funding cycles
  • • Research independence and flexibility
  • • Student training support
  • • Equipment and infrastructure funding
{/* SSHRC Insight Grants */}
SSHRC Insight Grants - Social Sciences Research
Up to $400K
Social Research
2-5 Years

Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council support for research excellence in social sciences, humanities, and arts, enabling researchers to pursue original investigations.

Research Disciplines:
  • • Social sciences and humanities
  • • Arts and creative research
  • • Policy and governance studies
  • • Cultural and societal research
Support Included:
  • • Research assistant funding
  • • Travel and fieldwork support
  • • Knowledge mobilization
  • • Dissemination activities
{/* Mitacs Accelerate */}
Mitacs Accelerate - University-Industry Research
$15K per internship
Collaborative
4-12 months

Research internship program connecting companies with graduate students and postdoctoral fellows to work on innovation projects at early research stages.

Research Projects:
  • • Applied research problems
  • • Technology exploration
  • • Feasibility studies
  • • Market research and analysis
Program Benefits:
  • • Cost-share model (50/50)
  • • Access to university talent
  • • Flexible project duration
  • • Multiple internships possible
{/* CIHR Project Grants */}
CIHR Project Grants - Health Research
Up to $750K
Health Research
1-5 Years

Canadian Institutes of Health Research funding for health-related research projects spanning biomedical, clinical, and health systems research at early stages.

Research Categories:
  • • Biomedical research
  • • Clinical research
  • • Health services research
  • • Population health studies
Funding Features:
  • • Flexible project duration
  • • Open competition format
  • • Peer review process
  • • Knowledge translation support
shortAnswer: To apply for Stage 1: Ideation & Research Funding Canada 2026, start by reviewing the eligibility criteria and preparing a project proposal. Complete guide to Canadian ideation and research funding for early-stage innovation. Access up to $1M through NSERC Discovery Grants, university research programs, and basic R&D support for concept. Funding available: up to $1M.
shortAnswerQuestion: How can my business apply for Stage 1: Ideation & Research Funding Canada 2026 in 2026?

faq.question: What is the application deadline for Stage 1: Ideation & Research Funding Canada  | NSERC Discovery Grants | Up to  Early-Stage R&D in 2026?
faq.answer: Deadlines typically vary by specific program tier. However, for 2026, many initiatives operate on a rolling intake basis until funds are depleted. Check the official portal for specific cutoffs.

faq.question: How much funding can I get through Stage 1: Ideation & Research Funding Canada  | NSERC Discovery Grants | Up to  Early-Stage R&D?
faq.answer: Funding amounts range depending on business size and scope. Top awards can reach $1M.

faq.question: Do I have to give up equity for this program?
faq.answer: No. Government grants and subsidies are non-dilutive, meaning you retain 100% ownership of your company.

expertTip.title: Academic Partners
expertTip.content: Partner with a university researcher. NSERC grants are often more accessible when you have an academic collaboration.
